desc: Tbilisi is one of the most atmospheric and genuinely captivating cities in the world — a labyrinthine old town of balconied wooden houses, ancient sulfur bathhouses, and cave monasteries carved into volcanic rock. The country sits at the crossroads of Europe and Asia, and its culture is entirely its own: the Georgian alphabet, polyphonic folk music, and the world's oldest wine tradition (8,000 years of viticulture) make it unlike any other destination. Mtskheta, the ancient capital, and the cave city of Uplistsikhe are extraordinary. The Caucasus mountains rise dramatically behind the city. All of this remains largely undiscovered — and exceptionally affordable.

todo.name: Old Town (Abanotubani) & sulfur baths
todo.desc: The ancient sulfur bath district has been a centre of Tbilisi life since the 5th century. Bathing in a domed private bath is an unmissable experience.

todo.name: Narikala Fortress & cable car
todo.desc: 4th-century fortress overlooking the old town — best reached by cable car for panoramic views over the terracotta rooftops and Mtkvari river.

todo.name: Mtskheta — ancient capital
todo.desc: Georgia's first capital and spiritual heart — a UNESCO World Heritage site with the magnificent Svetitskhoveli Cathedral. 20 min from Tbilisi.

todo.name: Uplistsikhe cave city
todo.desc: A 3,000-year-old cave city carved into volcanic rock above the Mtkvari river — one of the oldest settlements in the Caucasus. ~80 min from Tbilisi.

todo.name: Vardzia cave monastery
todo.desc: A 12th-century cliff-face monastery of 3,000 rooms carved into a single hillside — one of the great wonders of the medieval world. Day trip south.

todo.name: Georgian wine tasting
todo.desc: Georgia is the cradle of wine. Kakheti wine region produces world-class natural wines using ancient clay qvevri vessels — utterly different from anything in Europe.

todo.name: Kazbegi & Mount Kazbek
todo.desc: The road to Kazbegi is one of the world's great drives — ending at the iconic Gergeti Trinity Church perched above a glacial valley at 2,170 m.

todo.name: Sioni Cathedral & Metekhi Church
todo.desc: Two of Tbilisi's most ancient and beautiful churches, both dating to the 5th and 6th centuries, each with a dramatic riverside setting.

todo.name: Rustaveli Avenue & National Museum
todo.desc: The grand 19th-century boulevard lined with theatres and galleries. The National Museum houses extraordinary Scythian gold and ancient artefacts.

todo.name: Shota Rustaveli Opera House
todo.desc: One of the grandest opera houses in the Caucasus — stunning architecture and a lively cultural programme at a fraction of Western European prices.

wifi.note: Tbilisi has excellent internet infrastructure — one of the best in the region. Fast, reliable WiFi in cafes and hotels throughout the old town. A growing number of coworking spaces. No restrictions on internet access — all services available freely.

prac.fltNote: No direct Gatwick flight to Tbilisi (TBS). Best options: Wizz Air from London Luton (LTN) is direct; from Gatwick, connections typically route via Vienna, Warsaw, Riga or Istanbul. Turkish Airlines via Istanbul (IST) is a popular and well-priced option. Total journey 5–8 hrs.
prac.visa: Visa-free for UK citizens — up to 365 days
alerts: No direct Gatwick route — consider flying from Luton (Wizz Air direct) or connecting via Istanbul.

sweet: May, June and September are exceptional — warm and sunny with manageable crowds. Spring brings extraordinary blossom on the hillsides above the city.
